title: Kadsura roxburghiana
intro: Kadsura roxburghiana, also known as the winter cherry or kadam, is a woody climber or shrub native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Schisandraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Kadsura roxburghiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Kadsura roxburghiana is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into formulations such as capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Kadsura roxburghiana is a plant species native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. It is a member of the family Schisandraceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. The plant is also known to be a part of the order Austrobaileyales. Further classification of Kadsura roxburghiana places it in the genus Kadsura. This species is known for its medicinal and ornamental properties.
